title: (No AI) The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine

creator: Connie Loizos
title: (No AI) The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
dc:creator: Connie Loizos
content: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.
contentSnippet: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.

title: (No AI) I spy
author: Victoria Song
content:
![Senior reviewer Victoria Song wearing Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses while looking over her shoulder. The photo is lit so there are dramatic shadows across her face.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/10/257980_Meta_Ray-Ban_Display_AKrales_0111.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

I’m just doing my job, but turns out, it feels lousy holding other people’s privacy in your hands.

I've long argued that Hollywood has [simultaneously set](https://www.theverge.com/23678696/smart-glasses-spy-films-gadgets-privacy) and [ruined our expectations for smart glasses](https://gizmodo.com/video/how-science-fiction-ruined-smart-glasses). But after binge-watching two seasons of Netflix's _A Man on the Inside_, this is perhaps the first time I've seen Hollywood, perhaps inadvertently, illustrate the biggest cultural problem with smart glasses as they stand today.

In a nutshell, Ted Danson plays Charles Nieuwendyk, an elderly widower who finds a new purpose working for a private investigator. Armed with a pair of Ray-Ban Meta-like glasses, a voice recorder, and a smartphone, Nieuwendyk infiltrates a retirement home, and several privacy-infringing hijinks ensue as he hunts for t …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/column/961707/smart-glasses-ai-wearables-meta-surveillance-privacy)

Armed with a pair of Ray-Ban Meta-like glasses, a voice recorder, and a smartphone, Nieuwendyk infiltrates a retirement home, and several privacy-infringing hijinks ensue as he hunts for t …

title: (No AI) Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees
author: Tom Warren
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/gettyimages-2262967364.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Satya Nadella in February 2026. | Photo by Sven Hoppe / picture alliance via Getty Images

A year after cutting around [9,100 employees](https://www.theverge.com/news/693535/microsoft-layoffs-july-2025-xbox), Microsoft is making further layoffs today as it begins its new financial year. The software maker is laying off around 4,800 employees today, approximately 2.1 percent of its workforce. Most of the employees affected by today's cuts are in Microsoft's commercial sales business or the company's Xbox division.

In an internal memo to employees, Amy Coleman, executive vice president and Microsoft's chief people officer, blamed the job losses on a changing technology industry and the "need to adjust resources and roles and shift how we operate" to respond to how AI is impacting companies like Microsof …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/news/961528/microsoft-layoffs-july-2026-sales-xbox)

title: (No AI) How ChatGPT adoption has expanded
content: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.
contentSnippet: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.

title: (No AI) Core dump epidemiology: fixing an 18-year-old bug
content: OpenAI engineers used large-scale core dump analysis to debug rare infrastructure crashes, uncovering both a hardware fault and a long-standing software bug.
contentSnippet: OpenAI engineers used large-scale core dump analysis to debug rare infrastructure crashes, uncovering both a hardware fault and a long-standing software bug.

creator: Isabella Ward
title: (No AI) British Space Startup Launches Longevity Lab Into Orbit
dc:creator: Isabella Ward
content: The lab will beam back data to train AI models to predict how proteins behind age-related diseases like Alzheimer’s and certain cancers behave.
contentSnippet: The lab will beam back data to train AI models to predict how proteins behind age-related diseases like Alzheimer’s and certain cancers behave.
